zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Alice de Vaux於1409年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈洛登時，她的父親Nicholas Vaux27歲，母親Elizabeth27歲。她和Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1490年在英國英格蘭倫敦享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alice de Vaux于1409年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈洛登时，她的父亲Nicholas Vaux27岁，母亲Elizabeth27岁。她和Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1490年在英国英格兰倫敦享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alice de Vaux was born in 1409, in Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Nicholas Vaux, was 27 and her mother, Elizabeth, was 27. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton. She died in 1490, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Alice de Vaux est née en 1409, à Harrowden, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Nicholas Vaux, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton. Elle est décédée en 1490, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Alice de Vaux 1409 in Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Nicholas Vaux 27 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth 27. Sie hatte mit Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1490 im Alter von 81 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alice de Vaux è nata il nel 1409 nel Harrowden, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Nicholas Vaux, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton. Morì nel 1490, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alice de Vauxが1409年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーロウデンで生まれた時，父Nicholas Vauxは27歳，母Elizabethは27歳であった。彼女はSir Richard Sapcote of Eltonとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1490年にイギリスイギリスロンドンで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alice de Vaux(이)가 1409년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해로우든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Nicholas Vaux(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Alice de Vaux родилась в 1409 г. в местности харроуден, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Nicholas Vaux было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Sir Richard Sapcote of Elton. Она умерла в 1490 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Alice de Vaux nació en 1409, en Harrowden, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Nicholas Vaux, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Elizabeth, tenía 27 años.
